name: bind9-dnsutils
title: bind9-dnsutils
description:
Clients provided with BIND 9
The Berkeley Internet Name Domain (BIND 9) implements an Internet domain
name server.  BIND 9 is the most widely-used name server software on the
Internet, and is supported by the Internet Software Consortium, www.isc.org.
.
This package delivers various client programs related to DNS that are
derived from the BIND 9 source tree.
.
- dig - query the DNS in various ways
- nslookup - the older way to do it
- nsupdate - perform dynamic updates (See RFC2136)

commands.name: dnstap-read
commands.description: print dnstap data in human-readable form

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use delv because Clients provided with BIND 9.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use delv as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `delv  [@server]  [ [-4] | [-6] ] [-a anchor-file] [-b address] [-c class] [-d level] [-i] [-m]` as documented upstream for delv.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `delv [-h]` as documented upstream for delv.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `delv [-v]` as documented upstream for delv.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `delv [queryopt...] [query...]` as documented upstream for delv.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair delv with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `delv --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: delv - DNS lookup and validation utility
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install bind9 (1:9.20.24-1). Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `delv [queryopt...] [query...]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Clients provided with BIND 9.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Clients provided with BIND 9.
